slices.focus: Astros game scorekeeping as an unexpected mental reset
slices.summary: Spontaneously joined colleague Miguel for an Astros game and discovered that paper scorekeeping gave his mind clean, structured focus without feeling like labor. The evening was a genuine mental reset — the first time in a long stretch he fully unplugged from work. Plans occasional repeat outings with minimal preparation, keeping a pencil in the truck and limiting to one or two games per season piggybacking on downtown work days.
open_loops: Whether a second outing will feel as restorative as the first or lose the novelty effect
Finding more activities with a built-in structure and low-pressure company beyond baseball
Whether to eventually buy a proper scorebook if the habit proves consistent, or keep it deliberately minimal
decisions: Keep the first scorecard as a tangible memento of the experience
Keep a pencil in the truck for spontaneous future games when already downtown
Limit games to one or two per season, piggybacking on existing downtown work days to keep it spontaneous
